subject: Order #ORD-5500 - Multiple severe issues, requesting escalation
description:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing on behalf of Nexus Corp (enterprise account ENT-4400) regarding order #ORD-5500, a bulk purchase of 25 units placed on February 15th.

The problems with this order are unacceptable:

1. WRONG ITEMS: We ordered 10x Model-A Pro Monitors (27-inch, 4K) but received 10x Model-A Basic Monitors (24-inch, 1080p). These are completely different products. Item IDs: ITEM-501 through ITEM-510.

2. DAMAGED ITEMS: 5 of the 10x USB-C Docking Stations (ITEM-511 through ITEM-515) arrived with cracked housings. The packaging was visibly crushed.

3. MISSING ITEMS: 5x Wireless Keyboard + Mouse combos (ITEM-516 through ITEM-520) were listed on the packing slip but NOT in the boxes. They are simply missing.

4. OVERCHARGE: We were charged $47,500.00 but the agreed enterprise pricing was $42,750.00. That is a $4,750.00 overcharge which violates our contract terms.

5. LATE DELIVERY: The order was promised by February 20th (per our SLA) but didn't arrive until March 3rd -- 11 days late. This caused our new office setup to be delayed by two weeks, costing us significant productivity.

This is absolutely unacceptable for an enterprise account paying premium rates. We need:
- Immediate replacement of all wrong monitors with the correct Model-A Pro units
- Full refund for the damaged docking stations ($2,250.00)
- Immediate shipment of the missing keyboard/mouse combos
- The $4,750.00 overcharge corrected
- Compensation for the late delivery
- I want this escalated to a manager. If this is not resolved within 48 hours we will be reviewing our contract.

Regards,
Michael Torres
IT Procurement Director, Nexus Corp
